_comment: FAIR cross-tool comparison spec for `fulcrum compare` / `fulcrum audit`.
Everything is GENERIC: name your tools tool-a/tool-b/... and point each at a
real binary on PATH. NO competitor product name belongs in this file — the
harness only needs the binary + an argv template.
- {threads} the concrete thread count for the cell (or use thread_arg/auto_arg)
How the five benchmark-inflation holes are closed BY CONSTRUCTION:
#1 each tool's binary is probed (native vs interpreter-shim) and its
per-invocation startup is measured and SUBTRACTED. version_arg names the
cheap startup probe ('--version' default; '' means run bare).
#2 give each tool its DOCUMENTED BEST config: thread_arg is the per-N flag
spelling (e.g. '-T{n}'), auto_arg is the all-cores/auto flag (e.g. '-T0').
#3 every run's output sha256 is checked against the reference decoder below;
a mismatch DISQUALIFIES the cell (fast-but-wrong never wins).
#4 list multiple corpora (different 'kind's) and thread cells; the report
gives the HONEST SCOPE across the whole matrix, not one cell.
#5 runs are interleaved best-of-N with a contention + data-quality guard.
subject: tool-a
